title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Thank interest contributing heteroskedasticity_identification R package! document provides guidelines workflows contributing.

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: follow GitHub Flow branching strategy, simple effective R package development.

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: main - stable, production-ready branch. releases made branch. Feature branches - Short-lived branches developing new features fixes.

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Use descriptive branch names prefixes: feature/ - New features (e.g., feature/add-robust-variance) fix/ - Bug fixes (e.g., fix/issue-42) docs/ - Documentation updates (e.g., docs/improve-vignette) test/ - Test additions improvements (e.g., test/coverage-improvement)

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
code: # Fork the repository on GitHub, then: git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/heteroskedasticity_identification.git cd heteroskedasticity_identification git remote add upstream https://github.com/fernando-duarte/heteroskedasticity_identification.git

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
code: # Install R package dependencies Rscript -e "devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE)"  # Install pre-commit (requires Python) pip install pre-commit pre-commit install  # Verify setup pre-commit run --all-files

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: project uses several modern development tools: Cursor AI: using Cursor, .cursorignore file prevents AI access sensitive files Docker: Optional .dockerignore file included containerized development Pre-commit hooks: Automated code quality checks

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
code: # Ensure your main branch is up to date git checkout main git pull upstream main  # Create a new branch for your feature git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Write clear, documented code following tidyverse style guide Add update tests new functionality Update documentation (roxygen2 comments, vignettes, NEWS.md) Ensure code passes checks

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
code: # Run checks locally before pushing devtools::check() devtools::test() devtools::document()  # Check code coverage (if covr is installed) covr::package_coverage()

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: project uses pre-commit hooks maintain code quality. hooks automatically: Format R code using styler (tidyverse style guide) Check code quality lintr Check spelling Ensure R code parsable Remove trailing whitespace fix file endings Sort .Rbuildignore entries Prevent committing large files common R artifacts manually run hooks:
code: # Run on staged files pre-commit run  # Run on all files pre-commit run --all-files  # Run specific hook pre-commit run styler --all-files

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: pre-commit hooks run automatically. make changes, review commit .
code: # Stage and commit with a clear message git add . git commit -m "Add feature: brief description of changes"

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: create pull request GitHub branch main.
code: # Push to your fork git push origin feature/your-feature-name

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Use clear, descriptive title: - ✅ “Add function White’s heteroskedasticity test” - ✅ “Fix: Correct variance calculation lewbel_estimator()” - ❌ “Update code” - ❌ “Fixed stuff”

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Include: - Summary changes - Related issue numbers (fixes #123) - Testing performed - breaking changes - Screenshots/examples applicable

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: submitting, ensure: - [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (pre-commit run ---files) - [ ] Code follows tidyverse style guide (enforced styler) - [ ] tests pass (devtools::test()) - [ ] R CMD check passes (devtools::check()) - [ ] Documentation updated - [ ] NEWS.md updated (user-facing changes) - [ ] Examples work correctly - [ ] sensitive information code comments

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: open PR, GitHub Actions automatically: - Run R CMD check multiple platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux) - Run tests coverage reporting - Check code coverage - Build documentation - Deploy pkgdown site (merged PRs) checks must pass merging.

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: follow tidyverse style guide. Key points: Use <- assignment (=) Use spaces around operators Use descriptive variable names (prefer heteroskedasticity_test het_test) Document exported functions roxygen2 Include examples documentation

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Write tests new functionality using testthat Test files named test-<functionality>.R Aim high test coverage (>80%) Test edge cases error conditions Use meaningful test descriptions Example test structure:
code: test_that("lewbel_estimator handles missing data correctly", {   # Test setup   data <- generate_test_data()    # Test expectation   expect_error(     lewbel_estimator(data_with_na),     "Data contains missing values"   ) })

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Use roxygen2 function documentation Include @examples exported functions Update vignettes major features Keep README.md current Document breaking changes NEWS.md Example documentation:
code: #' Estimate model using Lewbel (2012) method #' #' @param y Numeric vector of dependent variable #' @param X Matrix of explanatory variables #' @param Z Matrix of instruments (optional) #' #' @return A list of class "lewbel" containing estimates #' @export #' #' @examples #' data <- generate_lewbel_data(n = 100) #' fit <- lewbel_estimator(data$y, data$X) #' summary(fit)

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Understanding project structure helps navigate codebase:
code: . ├── R/                    # R source code ├── tests/               # Test files │   └── testthat/       # testthat tests ├── man/                # Documentation (generated) ├── vignettes/          # Long-form documentation ├── inst/               # Installed files ├── data/               # Package data (if any) ├── .github/            # GitHub Actions workflows └── ignore files:       # Development configuration     ├── .gitignore      # Git ignore patterns     ├── .Rbuildignore   # R build ignore patterns     ├── .cursorignore   # Cursor AI ignore patterns     └── .dockerignore   # Docker ignore patterns

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Releases managed maintainers: Features developed feature branches PRs merged main review Version bumped DESCRIPTION NEWS.md updated changes R CMD check passes platforms GitHub release created tag Package submitted CRAN (applicable) pkgdown site automatically updated

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: need help: - Check existing issues discussions - Review package documentation - Open new issue minimal reproducible example - Ask questions discussions bugs, please include: - R version OS - Minimal code reproduce issue - Expected vs actual behavior - error messages

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Please note project follows Contributor Code Conduct. participating project agree : - Use welcoming inclusive language - respectful differing viewpoints - Gracefully accept constructive criticism - Focus best community - Show empathy towards community members

title: Contributing to heteroskedasticity_identification
text: Key areas include: - Implementing core Lewbel (2012) functionality - Adding comprehensive test coverage - Creating user-friendly vignettes - Performance optimization large datasets detailed tasks priorities, check GitHub issues page. Thank contributing! 🎉

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: document provides comprehensive instructions using Docker hetid R package, implementing 2025 best practices containerization.

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Docker setup provides multiple environments: - Production: Minimal runtime package execution - Development: Full RStudio Server environment interactive development - Testing: Automated testing CI/CD - Simulation: Optimized Monte Carlo simulations

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Start RStudio Server interactive development:
code: # Start development environment ./docker/scripts/dev.sh start  # Access RStudio Server at http://localhost:8787 # Username: rstudio (no password required in dev mode)

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Build run production image:
code: # Build production image ./docker/scripts/build.sh  # Run package docker run --rm hetid:latest

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Execute Monte Carlo simulations:
code: # Run main simulation ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh run  # Run with custom parameters ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh run -n 500 -c 8 -m 16g

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Base: rocker/r-ver:4.5.0 Size: ~800MB (optimized) Purpose: Package execution simulations Security: Non-root user, minimal attack surface

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Base: rocker/rstudio:4.5.0 Size: ~2GB (includes development tools) Purpose: Interactive development RStudio Server Features: Full development toolchain, debugging tools

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Docker Engine 28.0+ (2025 version) 4GB RAM 2 CPU cores 10GB disk space

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: 8GB+ RAM 4+ CPU cores 20GB+ disk space SSD storage better /O performance

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Start Development Environment: Access RStudio Server: Open http://localhost:8787 browser Username: rstudio (password dev mode) Development Commands: Stop Environment:
code: ./docker/scripts/dev.sh start -d  # Start in background ./docker/scripts/dev.sh test     # Run package tests ./docker/scripts/dev.sh check    # Run R CMD check ./docker/scripts/dev.sh shell    # Open bash shell ./docker/scripts/dev.sh r-console # Open R console ./docker/scripts/dev.sh stop

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Build Production Image: Run Package Functions: Mount Data Volumes:
code: ./docker/scripts/build.sh -t production --tag-latest docker run --rm hetid:latest R -e "library(hetid); run_lewbel_demo()" docker run --rm \   -v $(pwd)/data:/app/data:ro \   -v $(pwd)/results:/app/results \   hetid:latest R -e "library(hetid); # your R code here"

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Run Different Simulation Types: Monitor Simulations: View Results:
code: # Main simulation ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh run -t main -n 1000  # Bootstrap demonstration ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh run -t bootstrap -n 100  # Sensitivity analysis ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh run -t sensitivity -n 500  # Sample size analysis ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh run -t sample -n 200 ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh status  # Check running simulations ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh logs    # Follow logs ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh results

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: complex workflows, use Docker Compose:
code: # Start all services docker-compose up -d  # Development with live reload docker-compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up  # Run specific service docker-compose run hetid-test  # Scale simulation workers docker-compose up --scale hetid-simulation=3

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: R_LIBS_USER: R library path OMP_NUM_THREADS: OpenMP thread count OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS: BLAS thread count DISABLE_AUTH: Disable RStudio authentication (dev )

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: /app/data: Input data (read-) /app/results: Output results /app/simulation-results: Simulation outputs /home/rstudio/hetid: Source code (development)

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Configure docker-compose.yml:
code: deploy:   resources:     limits:       cpus: '4.0'       memory: 8G     reservations:       cpus: '2.0'       memory: 4G

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Port 8787 already use: memory simulations: Permission issues volumes:
code: # Check what's using the port lsof -i :8787  # Use different port docker run -p 8788:8787 hetid:dev # Increase memory limit ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh run -m 16g  # Reduce simulation count ./docker/scripts/simulation.sh run -n 500 # Fix ownership sudo chown -R $(id -u):$(id -g) ./results

title: Docker Setup for hetid Package
text: Enable BuildKit caching: Use multi-stage builds: Optimize platform:
code: export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 ./docker/scripts/build.sh -t production --cache-from hetid:builder ./docker/scripts/build.sh -p linux/arm64  # For Apple Silicon

text: images run non-root users production Development mode disables authentication convenience Secrets mounted read-volumes Use specific version tags, avoid latest production

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: hetid package implements identification heteroskedasticity method Lewbel (2012) time-series models endogenous regressors. provides tools estimation inference traditional instruments available.

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: can install development version hetid GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("fernando-duarte/heteroskedasticity_identification")

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
code: heteroskedasticity_identification/ ├── R/                    # R functions implementing the methodology │   ├── messager.R       # Utility functions for messaging │   └── stopper.R        # Utility functions for error handling ├── lewbel2012/          # Implementation and analysis of Lewbel (2012) │   ├── lewbel2012.tex   # LaTeX source file │   ├── lewbel2012.pdf   # Compiled PDF document │   └── ...              # Supporting LaTeX files ├── tests/               # Unit tests │   └── testthat/        # testthat test files ├── vignettes/           # Package vignettes │   └── getting-started.Rmd ├── man/                 # Documentation (generated) ├── docs/                # pkgdown site (generated) ├── .github/             # GitHub Actions workflows │   └── workflows/ │       └── rworkflows.yml └── inst/                # Installed files     └── hooks/           # Pre-commit hook scripts

what: Calculate Set Identification Bounds for Lewbel Estimator — calculate_lewbel_bounds
title: Calculate Set Identification Bounds for Lewbel Estimator — calculate_lewbel_bounds
text: Computes set identification bounds endogenous parameter relaxed covariance restriction. Optionally computes bootstrap standard errors bounds.

title: Calculate Set Identification Bounds for Lewbel Estimator — calculate_lewbel_bounds
code: calculate_lewbel_bounds(data, tau, compute_se = FALSE, b_reps = 100)

title: Calculate Set Identification Bounds for Lewbel Estimator — calculate_lewbel_bounds
text: data Data.frame. Dataset containing Y1, Y2, Xk, Z variables. tau Numeric. Relaxation parameter covariance restriction (0 <= tau < 1). tau = 0, gives point identification. compute_se Logical. Whether compute bootstrap standard errors (default: FALSE). b_reps Integer. Number bootstrap replications compute_se = TRUE (default: 100).

title: Calculate Set Identification Bounds for Lewbel Estimator — calculate_lewbel_bounds
text: list containing: bounds: Numeric vector length 2 lower upper bounds se: Numeric vector length 2 bootstrap standard errors (requested)

title: Calculate Set Identification Bounds for Lewbel Estimator — calculate_lewbel_bounds
text: relaxed assumption |Corr(Z, \(\epsilon_1 \epsilon_2\))| <= tau |Corr(Z, \(\epsilon_2^2\))|, parameter gamma_1 set-identified. bounds computed real roots quadratic equation gamma_1.

title: Calculate Set Identification Bounds for Lewbel Estimator — calculate_lewbel_bounds
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ params <- list(   beta1_0 = 0.5, beta1_1 = 1.5, gamma1 = -0.8,   beta2_0 = 1.0, beta2_1 = -1.0,   alpha1 = -0.5, alpha2 = 1.0, delta_het = 1.2 ) # TODO: Update generate_lewbel_data call if its return columns change to # snake_case data <- generate_lewbel_data(1000, params)  # Point identification (tau = 0) bounds_point <- calculate_lewbel_bounds(data, tau = 0)  # Set identification with bootstrap SE bounds_set <- calculate_lewbel_bounds(   data,   tau = 0.2, compute_se = TRUE, b_reps = 100 ) } # }

what: Create Default Configuration for Lewbel Monte Carlo Simulations — create_default_config
title: Create Default Configuration for Lewbel Monte Carlo Simulations — create_default_config
text: Creates comprehensive configuration list parameters needed running Lewbel (2012) Monte Carlo simulations.

title: Create Default Configuration for Lewbel Monte Carlo Simulations — create_default_config
code: create_default_config(   num_simulations = 200,   main_sample_size = 500,   sample_sizes = c(250, 500, 1000, 2000),   delta_het = 0.8,   delta_het_values = c(0.4, 0.8, 1.2),   n_reps_by_n = 100,   n_reps_by_delta = 100,   bootstrap_reps = 100,   bootstrap_subset_size = 10,   bootstrap_demo_size = 5,   beta1_0 = 0.5,   beta1_1 = 1.5,   gamma1 = -0.8,   beta2_0 = 1,   beta2_1 = -1,   alpha1 = -0.5,   alpha2 = 1,   tau_set_id = 0.2,   endog_var_name = "Y2",   exog_var_names = "Xk" )

title: Create Default Configuration for Lewbel Monte Carlo Simulations — create_default_config
text: num_simulations Integer. Number main simulation runs (default: 1000). main_sample_size Integer. Primary sample size main results (default: 1000). sample_sizes Integer vector. Sample sizes consistency analysis (default: c(500, 1000, 2000)). delta_het Numeric. Heteroscedasticity strength parameter (default: 1.2). delta_het_values Numeric vector. Delta values sensitivity analysis (default: c(0.4, 0.8, 1.2)). n_reps_by_n Integer. Replications per sample size (default: 100). n_reps_by_delta Integer. Replications per delta value (default: 100). bootstrap_reps Integer. Number bootstrap replications (default: 100). bootstrap_subset_size Integer. Size bootstrap subset (default: 10). bootstrap_demo_size Integer. Size bootstrap demo (default: 5). beta1_0 Numeric. Intercept first equation (default: 0.5). beta1_1 Numeric. Slope first equation (default: 1.5). gamma1 Numeric. True value endogenous parameter (default: -0.8). beta2_0 Numeric. Intercept second equation (default: 1.0). beta2_1 Numeric. Slope second equation (default: -1.0). alpha1 Numeric. Factor loading first error (default: -0.5). alpha2 Numeric. Factor loading second error (default: 1.0). tau_set_id Numeric. Tau parameter set identification (default: 0.2). endog_var_name Character. Name endogenous variable (default: "Y2"). exog_var_names Character. Name exogenous variable (default: "Xk").

title: Create Default Configuration for Lewbel Monte Carlo Simulations — create_default_config
text: list containing configuration parameters.

title: Create Default Configuration for Lewbel Monte Carlo Simulations — create_default_config
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ config <- create_default_config() config$gamma1 # -0.8  # Custom configuration custom_config <- create_default_config(   num_simulations = 500,   gamma1 = -0.5 ) } # }

Sensitivity analysis results. bootstrap_examples Data.frame. Bootstrap examples. config List. Configuration object. verbose Logical. Whether print plots (default: TRUE).

what: Generate Data for Lewbel (2012) Triangular Model — generate_lewbel_data
title: Generate Data for Lewbel (2012) Triangular Model — generate_lewbel_data
text: Creates dataset based triangular model single-factor error structure satisfies Lewbel's identifying assumptions. data generating process uses common factor structure errors ensure covariance restriction Cov(Z, \(\epsilon_1 \epsilon_2\)) = 0 satisfied.

title: Generate Data for Lewbel (2012) Triangular Model — generate_lewbel_data
code: generate_lewbel_data(n_obs, params)

title: Generate Data for Lewbel (2012) Triangular Model — generate_lewbel_data
text: n_obs Integer. Sample size. params List. Parameters data generating process containing: beta1_0, beta1_1: Parameters first equation beta2_0, beta2_1: Parameters second equation gamma1: Endogenous parameter (key parameter interest) alpha1, alpha2: Factor loadings common factor U delta_het: Heteroscedasticity strength parameter

title: Generate Data for Lewbel (2012) Triangular Model — generate_lewbel_data
text: data.frame columns Y1, Y2, Xk, Z, epsilon1, epsilon2.

title: Generate Data for Lewbel (2012) Triangular Model — generate_lewbel_data
text: triangular model : $$Y_1 = \beta_{1,0} + \beta_{1,1}X + \gamma_1 Y_2 + \epsilon_1$$ $$Y_2 = \beta_{2,0} + \beta_{2,1}X + \epsilon_2$$ error structure follows single-factor model: $$\epsilon_1 = \alpha_1 U + V_1$$ $$\epsilon_2 = \alpha_2 U + V_2$$ U, V_1 independent standard normal, V_2 ~ N(0, exp(\(\delta\) Z)) Z = \(X^2 - E[X^2]\) heteroscedasticity driver.

title: Generate Data for Lewbel (2012) Triangular Model — generate_lewbel_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ params <- list(   beta1_0 = 0.5, beta1_1 = 1.5, gamma1 = -0.8,   beta2_0 = 1.0, beta2_1 = -1.0,   alpha1 = -0.5, alpha2 = 1.0, delta_het = 1.2 ) data <- generate_lewbel_data(1000, params) head(data) } # }

what: Run Complete Lewbel (2012) Monte Carlo Simulation — run_lewbel_monte_carlo
title: Run Complete Lewbel (2012) Monte Carlo Simulation — run_lewbel_monte_carlo
text: Executes comprehensive Monte Carlo simulation evaluate performance Lewbel's (2012) heteroscedasticity-based identification strategy. main function orchestrates simulation components.

title: Run Complete Lewbel (2012) Monte Carlo Simulation — run_lewbel_monte_carlo
code: run_lewbel_monte_carlo(   config = NULL,   run_verification = TRUE,   run_bootstrap_demo = TRUE,   run_sample_analysis = TRUE,   run_sensitivity = TRUE,   generate_plots = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run Complete Lewbel (2012) Monte Carlo Simulation — run_lewbel_monte_carlo
text: config List. Configuration object create_default_config(). NULL, uses default configuration. run_verification Logical. Whether run assumption verification (default: TRUE). run_bootstrap_demo Logical. Whether run bootstrap demonstration (default: TRUE). run_sample_analysis Logical. Whether run sample size analysis (default: TRUE). run_sensitivity Logical. Whether run sensitivity analysis (default: TRUE). generate_plots Logical. Whether generate visualization plots (default: TRUE). verbose Logical. Whether print progress results (default: TRUE).

title: Run Complete Lewbel (2012) Monte Carlo Simulation — run_lewbel_monte_carlo
text: list containing: config: Configuration used results_main: Main simulation results results_by_n: Sample size analysis results (run) results_by_delta: Sensitivity analysis results (run) bootstrap_demo: Bootstrap demonstration results (run) analysis: Summary analysis plots: Visualization plots (generated)

title: Run Complete Lewbel (2012) Monte Carlo Simulation — run_lewbel_monte_carlo
text: function runs complete Monte Carlo evaluation including: Verification Lewbel's identifying assumptions Main simulation comparing OLS vs 2SLS (Lewbel) estimators Bootstrap demonstration set identification bounds Sample size consistency analysis Sensitivity analysis heteroscedasticity strength Comprehensive result analysis visualization simulation uses parallel processing efficiency includes proper seed management reproducibility.

title: Run Complete Lewbel (2012) Monte Carlo Simulation — run_lewbel_monte_carlo
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run with default settings results <- run_lewbel_monte_carlo()  # Run with custom configuration custom_config <- create_default_config(   num_simulations = 500,   gamma1 = -0.5,   delta_het = 1.5 ) results <- run_lewbel_monte_carlo(custom_config)  # Run only main simulation without extras results <- run_lewbel_monte_carlo(   run_bootstrap_demo = FALSE,   run_sample_analysis = FALSE,   run_sensitivity = FALSE ) } # }

what: Verify Lewbel's Key Identifying Assumptions — verify_lewbel_assumptions
title: Verify Lewbel's Key Identifying Assumptions — verify_lewbel_assumptions
text: Tests whether data generating process satisfies key assumptions required Lewbel's (2012) identification strategy. includes testing covariance restriction instrument relevance condition.

title: Verify Lewbel's Key Identifying Assumptions — verify_lewbel_assumptions
code: verify_lewbel_assumptions(   data = NULL,   config = NULL,   n_obs = 10000,   params = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Verify Lewbel's Key Identifying Assumptions — verify_lewbel_assumptions
text: data Data.frame. Optional. Pre-generated data verify (alternative n_obs/params). config List. Optional. Configuration object (used data parameter). n_obs Integer. Sample size verification (default: 10000, used params). params List. Parameters DGP (format generate_lewbel_data). verbose Logical. Whether print detailed output (default: TRUE).

title: Verify Lewbel's Key Identifying Assumptions — verify_lewbel_assumptions
text: Invisibly returns list verification results data.

title: Verify Lewbel's Key Identifying Assumptions — verify_lewbel_assumptions
text: function tests: Assumption A2: Cov(Z, \(\epsilon_1 \epsilon_2\)) = 0 (covariance restriction) Assumption A3: Cov(Z, \(\epsilon_2^2\)) != 0 (instrument relevance) Endogeneity: Cov(\(\epsilon_1\), \(\epsilon_2\)) != 0 Can called two ways: verify_lewbel_assumptions(data, config) - using pre-generated data verify_lewbel_assumptions(n_obs = 10000, params = params) - generating new data

title: Verify Lewbel's Key Identifying Assumptions — verify_lewbel_assumptions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ config <- create_default_config() params <- list(   beta1_0 = config$beta1_0, beta1_1 = config$beta1_1, gamma1 = config$gamma1,   beta2_0 = config$beta2_0, beta2_1 = config$beta2_1,   alpha1 = config$alpha1, alpha2 = config$alpha2,   delta_het = config$delta_het ) verify_lewbel_assumptions(params = params) } # }
